characters.name: 8001 and 8002
characters.description: 8001 and 80028001 and 8002 with TsuraraPersonal statisticsSpeciesRobotLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Tsurara(immediate superior)Kanta Mizuno(boss)Current affiliation(s)West Oasis GovernmentCurrent team(s)Eighth ArmyCurrent occupation(s)Robot soldierAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 16,Chapter 1018001 and 8002are twoUndeadsoldiers who serve underTsurarain theEighth Army. The two robots have the typical appearance of Undead soldiers, which is that of a completely tall skinny robot with an all-black combat uniform and one glowing eye on their face. At the beginning of theWar between the West and South Oases, theEighth Armywas formed as a response to the siege of theD-3 Resource Mining Village.[1]Three years and several months into the war, 8001 and 8002 began to work under Tsurara to track down theteru teru bōzu. This was because theteru teru bōzuwere deemed to be terrorists not affiliated with any side in the war but consistently helpedSouth Oasis Governmentsoldiers as well as theanti-West factionwhich opposedŌtaki's leadership in the West Oasis. About a day after the South Oasis Army's air raid on the14th Strategic Hamlet,TaikoandAkiofought againstUshio Mizusaki, who detonated someC-4and caused a large portion of the underground ruins to collapse.[2]When Ushio was knocked off a ledge by Akio, one of the Undead soldiers rescued him and put an IV in his arm since they were ordered to recover him as aHulonsample.[3]

characters.name: Akaike
characters.description: AkaikeEnglish nameAkagiKanji name赤池Voice actorsJapaneseDai MatsumotoEnglishPhil ParsonsPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)ŌnamiF-Type mercenaryPrevious affiliation(s)Uzumaki Chemicals(shut down)Previous occupation(s)Fisheye Specialsuit testerAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 7,Chapter 39Debut (anime)Frustration and DespairAkaike(赤池), alternatively known asAkagiin the English dub, is an employee ofUzumaki Chemicalswho was assigned to captureSeiji Tanigawafor questioning. Akaike wears aFisheye SpecialE-Type suit, which has three red eyeholes on the mask arranged in an inverted triangle shape. He uses a Heckler & Koch G36E rifle for a firearm. He testedUzumaki Chemicals' prototype Fisheye suits with the help ofSeiji Tanigawa's engineering skills. After Tanigawa left the company and took confidential files as well as a C-Type suit with him, Akaike and two other men were ordered to go after him and capture him. Akaike brought an E-Type suit to combat Seiji's suit,Ōnamibrought a D-Type suit, andanother mandonned an F-Type suit. All three managed to track down Seiji and also found him withJunko Asagirihiding in a cave. They gave the two ten minutes to give up or they would be killed. Tanigawa fled the cave and briefly held off the men.[1]

characters.name: Akio Kawaguchi
characters.description: Akio KawaguchiAnimeMangaSuitKanji name川口 秋夫Voice actorsJapaneseOtoya KawanoEnglishJustin CookPersonal statisticsAge19 (beginning of story)[1]23 (end of war)[2]GenderMaleHair colorBrownSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Fuyuo KawaguchiandHaruo KawaguchiIkemitsuCurrent affiliation(s)West Oasis Handyman Guild,Reverse Handyman GuildCurrent team(s)B TeamCurrent occupation(s)MercenaryAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 1,Chapter 4Debut (anime)Tank and Machine GunsAkio Kawaguchi(川口秋夫Kawaguchi Akio) is the middle brother of the threeMachine Gun Brothersand a sibling ofNatsuko Kawaguchi. He uses a M249 SAW machine gun as his weapon. 23 (end of war)[2] Akio is a 19-year-old[1]man with shaggy brown hair and a small scar on the bottom right of his chin. He is the second oldest of his siblings, with the oldest beingFuyuo, and his younger siblings are Natsuko andHaruo. Like his other brothers, he wears the sunshade ofMugen-Ya'sHandyman Guildand it has the kanji symbol for the number two (二), being the middle brother. He usually wears a white mask and a canteen around his neck. Under his mercenary clothes, he wears an orange shirt and dark green pants. He is muscular but not as much as his older brother.

characters.name: Amami
characters.description: AmamiPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent affiliation(s)South Oasis GovernmentCurrent occupation(s)South Oasis soldierAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 14,Chapter 87Amami(天海) is aSouth Oasissoldier who almost got captured by theWest Oasis Armybut was rescued by theteru teru bōzu. Amami is an adult man with a skinny build, pale skin, and short dark hair. He wears light clothing consisting of a long-sleeved shirt and pants. He has a wife and child that he does not see often due to theWest–South war.[1]Three years into this war,Natsuko KawaguchiordersTaiko KoizumiandMitsuru Koidemizuto work with the South Oasis to combat the current Western Government. The two of them meet Amami, and their task is to help with carrying supplies and escort Amami if there is somehow a battle. Amami holds contempt for the two since they are moles who only care about money, but nonetheless works with them due to their reputation.

characters.name: Asari
characters.description: AsariAnimeMangaVoice actorsJapaneseAsami WaragaiEnglishAmy RosenthalPersonal statisticsGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)ShijimiCurrent occupation(s)ThiefAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 4,Chapter 22Debut (anime)Ideals and Reality Asari(あさり) is the older sister ofShijimi, and the two of them are known as theSympathy Sisters. Both sisters are named after two types of clams. They are orphans who pretend they are on the verge of dying to gain sympathy from nearby travelers. When travelers try to help them, the sisters steal their food, water, and other supplies, resulting in the death of the travelers. Asari is a young girl with short brown hair and gray eyes. She is taller than her sister, and she wears a tan hoodie. Underneath she has a tank top which appears baggy due to her malnutrition.

characters.name: Asari
characters.description: AsariAnimeMangaVoice actorsJapaneseAsami WaragaiEnglishAmy RosenthalPersonal statisticsGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)ShijimiCurrent occupation(s)ThiefAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 4,Chapter 22Debut (anime)Ideals and Reality Asari(あさり) is the older sister ofShijimi, and the two of them are known as theSympathy Sisters. characters.name: Ayukawa
characters.description: AyukawaAnimeMangaEnglish nameAyukaKanji name鮎川Voice actorsJapaneseTakahiro ImamuraEnglishJason DouglasPersonal statisticsSpeciesRobotLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Kanta Mizuno(boss)Sawada(boss)Samesuga(partner)Current affiliation(s)West Oasis GovernmentCurrent team(s)8th Army(manga only)Current occupation(s)Robot soldierAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 10,Chapter 58Debut (anime)Frustration and DespairAyukawa(鮎川), shortened toAyukain the English dub, is a humanoid robot fighting against theanti-government factionwithin theWest Oasis Government. He andSamesugawork as a team and take orders fromSawada. Ayukawa is twice the size of a normal human and has glowing purple eyes, with the left eye being slightly bigger. He wears a dome-shaped sunshade on his head and wears a dark grey coat, dark gloves and boots. His appearance and size is exactly like Samesuga, with the only difference being the eyes. In the manga, Ayukawa's eyes are the same size. Just like theGuardiansof theDark Ages, Ayukawa is not harmed by bullets and can only sustain damage from more powerful weapons like RPGs and anti-tank weaponry. In the manga, both Ayukawa and Samesuga carry machetes with them, as do other robot soldiers. Ayukawa does not use any weapons in the anime, whereas in the manga he uses a Heckler & Koch VP70 pistol. In the anime, both Ayukawa and Samesuga are rather incompetent when it comes to quick thinking since they claim they are initially not programmed to respond to preemptive attacks. Nevertheless, Ayukawa's strength is quite good and he is taught martial arts for better combat. In the manga, in contrast, Ayukawa has a more human personality. Throughout the desert, he is known to help people in need on his travels, including walking into a burning building to save some children and stopping a man from punching a child.

characters.name: Big Man
characters.description: Big ManAnimeMangaKanji name大きな男Voice actorsJapaneseKōhei FukuharaPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Little ManAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 6,Chapter 34Debut (anime)Dog Woman and RockTheBig Man(大きな男Ōkina otoko) is muscle-for-hire who was one of several people who attempted and failed to destroy theInumeiwa. The big man is a young tall man with a muscular body who wears purple pants, a belt, and no shirt. His hair is spiky and blonde in the anime, and in the manga his hair is short and dark. He hails from the West Oasis of theGreat Kanto Desert, and he is friends with thelittle man. When the land speculatorMurahamahad trouble removing the cursed rock known as the Inumeiwa, he hired several people to destroy the rock for 500,000 Yen, including the big man. One day, the big man went with the little man to the rock, and the big man proudly stated that destroying the rock would be a piece of cake. He got his pickaxe ready and tried to launch it forward, but the pickaxe fell backward from his hands, the pointed edge piercing the little man's skull. Shocked, he panicked and was freaked out by the Inumeiwa's curse.[1]

characters.name: Daikichi Tagame
characters.description: Daikichi TagamePersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent occupation(s)Land mogulAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 1,Chapter 2 Daikichi Tagame(田亀大吉Tagame Daikichi) is a rich land mogul in the East Oasis. He has a daughter namedKichiko. Daikichi is very arrogant due to his wealth, and he is very controlling of his daughter's life. For example, when she ran away from him to be with her boyfriendTamō, he hiredDesert Punkto get her back. After Desert Punk captures her and brings her back to him, Daikichi slaps his daughter for running away, and tells her that desert men like her boyfriend are disgusting and filthy, and he accuses Kanta of not brushing his teeth or taking baths (since only the rich can afford baths). Daikichi is very arrogant due to his wealth, and he is very controlling of his daughter's life. For example, when she ran away from him to be with her boyfriendTamō, he hiredDesert Punkto get her back. After Desert Punk captures her and brings her back to him, Daikichi slaps his daughter for running away, and tells her that desert men like her boyfriend are disgusting and filthy, and he accuses Kanta of not brushing his teeth or taking baths (since only the rich can afford baths). When Tamō returns to take Kichiko back, he is very stingy with the amount of money he is willing to re-hire Kanta to stop Tamō and his men. He thinks of his daughter as a well-behaved girl, but she retorts that she is grownup. Daikichi is angry and tells himself that he never makes any wrong decisions due to his wealth, showing he is very conceited. Because of his treatment towards his daughter and his inability to understand her feelings, she runs away permanently.

characters.name: Dog Woman
characters.description: Dog WomanAnimeMangaRomanji name (manga)InumeRomanji name (anime)Inuon'naVoice actorsJapaneseKumi SakumaEnglishCynthia CranzPersonal statisticsAgeThousands of yearsGenderFemaleHair colorMahagonySpeciesSpiritAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 6,Chapter 34Debut (anime)Dog Woman and Rock TheDog Woman(犬女Inume) was a spirit who inhabited a rock called theInumeiwa, which the people of theGreat Kanto Desertbelieve is cursed. She is the antagonist ofepisode 8(Chapter 34). The Dog Woman is an attractive female spirit with dog-themed clothing. In the manga, she has a ponytail on her hair, but in the anime her dog hood covers up most of her hair except for her bangs. She wears thigh boots, a small dress, and a dog collar around her neck. A fluffy dog tail is seen on her tailbone in addition.

characters.name: Doronuma
characters.description: DoronumaFacePolice uniformKanji name土露沼Personal statisticsGenderMaleHair colorBlackSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent affiliation(s)West Oasis Police,Reverse Handyman GuildCurrent occupation(s)Police officerAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 6,Chapter 33Debut (anime)Frustration and DespairDoronuma(土露沼) is a police officer and a member of theanti-government faction. He provides information to the group while working with theWest Oasis Governmentfrom the inside. Doronuma is a middle-aged man of average height with a short, black bowl cut. He always wears a sunshade and a satchel around his shoulders. His police uniform consists of heavy clothing with multiple pockets of supplies, and his police helmet has a triangle symbol with a circle inside. He uses a Colt Delta Elite for his firearm. Doronuma is a corrupt man who is willing to work with criminals until they are no longer useful to him. He treats his partners nicely and he appears to be close toDesert Punk, but how or why they became friends is unknown. Doronuma is a corrupt man who is willing to work with criminals until they are no longer useful to him. He treats his partners nicely and he appears to be close toDesert Punk, but how or why they became friends is unknown.

characters.name: Edogawa Gang
characters.description: The four members of the Edogawa Gang TheEdogawa Gang(江戸川組Edogawa-gumi), also known as theEdo River Gang, is a criminal gang of thieves in theGreat Kanto Desert. Their main activities involve seizing control of villages that strike water so they can make money off them. The gang is comprised of four members who all wear and use the same gear. All members use Soviet RPD light machine guns (and one is seen with a Valmet M82 rifle), and they wear dark grey clothing underneath their tan tunics. They have a tan headscarf wrapped around their heads and faces, and covering their eyes are green-tinted visors, which are part of the Kirisame Type-100 Helmets they wear. An important piece of gear they use are the rockets on their backs that let them fly at high speeds to succeed in ambushing villages.

characters.name: Edogawa Gang

Their main activities involve seizing control of villages that strike water so they can make money off them. The gang is comprised of four members who all wear and use the same gear. All members use Soviet RPD light machine guns (and one is seen with a Valmet M82 rifle), and they wear dark grey clothing underneath their tan tunics. They have a tan headscarf wrapped around their heads and faces, and covering their eyes are green-tinted visors, which are part of the Kirisame Type-100 Helmets they wear. An important piece of gear they use are the rockets on their backs that let them fly at high speeds to succeed in ambushing villages.

characters.name: Ehato
characters.description: EhatoKanji name江波戸Personal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Natsuko Kawaguchi(boss)HimuroCurrent affiliation(s)Anti-mainstream factionCurrent occupation(s)SoldierAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 10,Chapter 58Ehato(江波戸) is aWest Oasis Governmentsoldier who served underNatsuko Kawaguchi's command and was allied with theanti-mainstream faction. Ehato wears a helmet with a visor, a hooded cloak, and soldier armor underneath. He carries a PRC Type-56 rifle for a weapon. WhenKaoru KaizukaassignedNatsuko Kawaguchito retrieve records from theDark Agesinunderground ruins, Ehato and other soldiers under her command accessed the underground ruins by massacring the people in the 25th Settlement Village and traveled about ten kilometers underground east of the village.[1]Although the group retrieved the records, several robot andcyborg soldiersattempted to kill them. The cyborgs killed eight of the soldiers[2]and injured their comradeHimuro, so the surviving soldiers created a makeshift hideout in the ruins. As Himuro had his wounds treated, Ehato and the others deterred the enemies from pursuing them further by planting bombs throughout the ruins, which the robots were ordered to protect per theWest Oasis Government's wishes.

characters.name: Eiji
characters.description: EijiKanji name泳児Personal statisticsGenderMaleHair colorBlackSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Narumi the Ripper(boss)TeshigawaraSuigetsuNamihara[1]Previous partner(s)Unnamed younger sister[2]Ikewaki couple (adopted parents)[2]Current affiliation(s)West Oasis GovernmentCurrent occupation(s)Soldier (militiaman)AppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 13,Chapter 79Eiji(泳児) is aWest Oasis Armysoldier who fights for the West underNarumi the Ripper's militia. Eiji is a skinny man with pale skin and short black hair. He wears In his youth, Eiji and his younger sister were adopted by the Ikewaki couple after they lost their parents. However, their foster parents constantly demeaned and punished both children. Additionally, the two parents sold Eiji's younger sister into slavery due to their poverty, also taunting the boy as his sister was taken away. As a result, Eiji became so angry that he killed his own parents by beating them to death. When the town found out about the murder, they gathered in a mob and demanded to know what happened, but he claimed that his own best friend was responsible. He got away with the crime whereas his best friend was murdered in a lynching.[2]

characters.name: Elder
characters.description: ElderVoice actorsJapaneseAkira KushidaEnglishJ. Paul SlavensPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanAppearancesDebut (anime)Sand and Rain TheElder(長老Chōrō) is a villager in the desert who sings the themes of multiple characters. He is first seen in Water Station 22, where he lives with a younger relative. He is an old man with blonde hair and carries a walking cane with him at all times. He wears an orange hood with a crescent moon necklace around his neck.

characters.name: F-Type mercenary
characters.description: F-Type mercenaryKanji name渦巻化成の傭兵Voice actorsJapaneseTakayasu UsuiEnglishDavid Wilson-BrownPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)ŌnamiAkaikePrevious affiliation(s)Uzumaki Chemicals(shut down)Current occupation(s)Fisheye Specialsuit testerAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 7,Chapter 39Debut (anime)Frustration and DespairTheF-Type mercenary, credited asUzumaki Chemicals mercenary(渦巻化成の傭兵Uzumaki Kasei no yōhei) in the anime, is an unnamed employee ofUzumaki Chemicalswho was assigned to captureSeiji Tanigawafor questioning. He wears a tan F-TypeFisheye Specialsuit with four red eyeholes on the mask arranged in a square shape. He carries an H&K GR9C light machine gun for a weapon. WhenUzumaki Chemicalsbegan producingFisheye Specialsuits using illicitDark Agetechnology, he was one of several people to test out the different suit models and abilities, including their firing range and enemy detection.[1]One of the company's engineers,Seiji Tanigawa, fled with confidential files and a C-Type suit, so the F-Type mercenary was ordered to pursue and capture him for questioning. Going into thedesertwithŌnamiandAkaike, the three men followed the signal from the files' emitter and eventually found Tanigawa hiding in a cave withJunko Asagiri. Ōnami gave them ten minutes to surrender or else they would kill them, prompting the two fugitives to quickly prepare.[2]

characters.name: Furio Ōshimo
characters.description: Furio ŌshimoKanji name大霜振男Personal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent occupation(s)Leader of thugsAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 3,Chapter 13Furio Ōshimo(大霜振男Ōshimo Furio) was the leader of a group of bandits who killed the bounty hunterKatsutaka Ikenonaka. Ōshimo is a tall, bald man with a cleft chin and thin eyes. He wears a hooded jacket and pants with multiple pockets around the waist. He and his thugs use Simonov SKS rifles for their firearms. Ōshimo's group hid out in an abandoned mine south of a town, and Ōshimo had a small bounty on his head. The bounty hunterKatsutaka Ikenonakaand his apprenticeTsuraratracked down Ōshimo, but they were outnumbered and ran. Ikenonaka got injured as he ran and Tsurara intentionally left him behind, and Ōshimo's thugs killed him. Soon, Tsurara got a revenge killing permit from the North Oasis to kill Ōshimo, and the apprentice hiredDesert PunkandKosunato help him.

characters.name: Furuido
characters.description: FuruidoPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHuman (Hulon)Loyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Tsurara8001 and 8002Current affiliation(s)Eighth ArmyCurrent occupation(s)TrackerAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 19,Chapter 115Furuido(古井戸), nicknamedGun Dog(ガンドッグ), was aHulonknown for his superhuman ability to track people via their smell. During thewar between West and South, he worked for theWest Oasis Governmentto track down people, eventually working withTsurarato track theteru teru bōzu. Furuido wears a baggy hood over his head and a metal helmet that covers his cranium and eyes. The helmet has two small holes for the eyes. He is of average height and has short dark hair and a wide nose. He carried an M3 submachine gun for a firearm. After Tsurara's 8th Army is given permission to track theteru teru bōzu, Tsurara has Furuido travel with him and twocyborg soldiersto track the enemy's scent. As they travel, they come across a small hole whereTaiko KoizumiandAkio Kawaguchiwent through. Furuido confirms they went through but they cannot go through it, so they have to go around, remarking that he can track people's scents lasting up to a week. He and Tsurara must be 1,000 meters away from Taiko at all times since she can detect them within that range.

characters.name: Fuyuo Kawaguchi
characters.description: Fuyuo KawaguchiAnimeMangaSuitEnglish name (anime)FuyoKanji name川口冬夫Voice actorsJapaneseJirō SaitōEnglishJeremy InmanPersonal statisticsAge20[1]GenderMaleHair colorBlackSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Akio KawaguchiandHaruo KawaguchiCurrent affiliation(s)West Oasis Handyman Guild,Reverse Handyman GuildCurrent occupation(s)MercenaryAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 1,Chapter 4Debut (anime)Tank and Machine GunsFuyuo Kawaguchi(川口冬夫Kawaguchi Fuyuo), shortened toFuyoin the English dub, is one of theMachine Gun Brothersand the eldest sibling, and his younger sister isNatsuko Kawaguchi. Fuyuo is a 20-year-old man[1]with a tall and muscular stature. He has two scars on his face, one horizontal scar below his eyes across his nose and one vertical scar on his left temple. He wears a light brown desert poncho around his torso that also encircles his head, and he wears a white mask. While he does have a sunshade with theWest Oasis Handyman Guildinsignia on it (and the kanji symbol for one (一) inside the circle), he virtually never wears it, unlike his two brothers. His weapon of choice is a M2 Browning .50 caliber machine gun with rifle stock and forward grip, which he carries with a sling belt on his right side.

characters.name: Gendō Koidemizu
characters.description: Gendō KoidemizuAnimeMangaEnglish nameKoidVoice actorsJapaneseToshihiko NakajimaEnglishBill JenkinsPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent affiliation(s)West Oasis Handyman GuildCurrent occupation(s)MercenaryAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 9,Chapter 49Debut (anime)Junko and Pursuer Gendō Koidemizu(小出水厳道Koidemizu Gendō) is a mercenary who works for theWest Oasis Handyman GuildinWater Station 30. He is the tenth and last mentor toDesert Punk, and he has a son namedMitsuruwhom he is training to become a mercenary like him. Gendō is a tall man with black hair, and wears a desert poncho with the helmet of his mercenary guild. He is very calm and caring, as both Kanta and Taiko have noted his generosity.

characters.name: Gendō Koidemizu

characters.name: Giwa Seto
characters.description: Giwa SetoKanji name瀬戸際Personal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent affiliation(s)Southern Neighborhood Association (14th Strategic Hamlet)Current occupation(s)Southern workerAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 17,Chapter 104 Giwa Seto(瀬戸際Seto Giwa) is a Southerner inWest Oasis Strategic Hamlet 14. He acts as infiltration for theanti-West Government factionto keep tabs on Southerners and the West Oasis security police. Seto is a tall, bald man with a South symbol (南) tattooed on his forehead to designate hisSouthernbackground. Like other Southern workers in his district, he wears a light hoodie and pants.

characters.name: Gorou Karuizawa
characters.description: Gorou KaruizawaPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent affiliation(s)South Oasis GovernmentCurrent occupation(s)New South Government soldierAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 20,Chapter 119 Gorou Karuizawa(軽井沢五郎Karuizawa Gorō) is a soldier for thenew South Oasis Governmentand the sole survivor of theD-3siege. He is the central character in volumes 20 and 21. Karuizawa is a young adult male with a frail build. He has short black hair but later had it covered with a light-colored hoodie.

characters.name: Gorou Karuizawa

characters.name: Guardian
characters.description: GuardianAnimeMangaAlternate namesGatekeeperVoice actorsJapaneseTaro MasuokaEnglishJim JohnsonPersonal statisticsHeightBetween 2 and 3 metersSpeciesRobotAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 3,Chapter 16Debut (anime)Life and Game AGuardian(番人Ban'nin) is a humanoid robot created sometime near the end of theDark Agesto serve as a substitute for human soldiers. By itself, one Guardian has the strength of one hundred men, and one army of Guardians can take on a human battalion with ease. Due to their dominance, they were believed to have caused the near extinction of civilization centuries before the modern desert era. The most prominent Guardian is the one which guarded the85th skeleton city.

characters.name: Guardian

characters.name: Haro Ōnami
characters.description: Haro ŌnamiVoice actorsJapaneseAtsushi ImaruokaEnglishBill Flynn (episode 1)Christopher Bevins (episode 8 onward)Personal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Tsunami ŌnamiAppearancesDebut (anime)Demon and Boobies Christopher Bevins (episode 8 onward) Haro Ōnami(大波 波浪Ōnami Haro) is a recurring character who only appears in theDesert Punkanime. Haro is the father ofNamikoand husband ofTsunami. He first appears inDemon and Boobiesbeing chased by members of theKawazu Clan, but is saved byDesert Punk. Unfortunately, Kanta doesn't do this out of the goodness of his heart, and takes 600,000 Yen from the family, saying that their lives were saved for 200,000 Yen each. He is then left with his family, crying that he wasn't strong enough to protect them and lost all their money. He and his family appear throughout the anime and travel around while his daughter remains hungry. Inepisode 21, it is shown that he is a vendor who sells some wares in a cart full of small bags. At the end of the anime, he and his family take cover from the Kawazu Clan once more.

characters.name: Haruo Kawaguchi
characters.description: Haruo KawaguchiAnimeMangaSuitEnglish name (anime)HaruVoice actorsJapaneseYasuhiro TakatoEnglishMike McFarlandPersonal statisticsAge17[1]GenderMaleHair colorBlackSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Akio KawaguchiandFuyuo KawaguchiCurrent affiliation(s)West Oasis Handyman Guild,Reverse Handyman GuildCurrent occupation(s)MercenaryAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 1,Chapter 4Debut (anime)Tank and Machine Guns Haruo Kawaguchi(川口春夫Kawaguchi Haruo), shortened toHaruin the English dub, is the youngest of the threeMachine Gun Brothersand the younger brother ofNatsuko Kawaguchi. He uses an M60 machine gun as his weapon. Haruo is a 17 year-old man and the youngest of the Kawaguchi siblings. He has short black hair and pale skin. Like his brothers, he wears the sunshade of theWest Oasis Handyman Guildwith the kanji for the number three (三), being the youngest brother. His mask differs from his brothers in that mask's eyes look like small blue dots inside a dark rectangular area. All three brothers wear dark purple gloves, but Akio and Haruo's gloves have one square patch on the back of the hand. Under his mercenary clothes, he wears a white shirt. Haruo is a 17 year-old man and the youngest of the Kawaguchi siblings. He has short black hair and pale skin. Like his brothers, he wears the sunshade of theWest Oasis Handyman Guildwith the kanji for the number three (三), being the youngest brother. His mask differs from his brothers in that mask's eyes look like small blue dots inside a dark rectangular area. All three brothers wear dark purple gloves, but Akio and Haruo's gloves have one square patch on the back of the hand. Under his mercenary clothes, he wears a white shirt. In addition, on his back he wears a banner advertising himself as a handyman (便利屋). In the anime, he adds a "Reverse" symbol on the top right (裏) to represent their new affiliation. Haruo is the most shy of the three brothers, and he speaks with a stutter.

characters.name: Haruo Kawaguchi
characters.description: Haruo KawaguchiAnimeMangaSuitEnglish name (anime)HaruVoice actorsJapaneseYasuhiro TakatoEnglishMike McFarlandPersonal statisticsAge17[1]GenderMaleHair colorBlackSpeciesHumanLoyalties and affiliationsCurrent partner(s)Akio KawaguchiandFuyuo KawaguchiCurrent affiliation(s)West Oasis Handyman Guild,Reverse Handyman GuildCurrent occupation(s)MercenaryAppearancesDebut (manga)Volume 1,Chapter 4Debut (anime)Tank and Machine Guns Haruo Kawaguchi(川口春夫Kawaguchi Haruo), shortened toHaruin the English dub, is the youngest of the threeMachine Gun Brothersand the younger brother ofNatsuko Kawaguchi. He uses an M60 machine gun as his weapon. Haruo is a 17 year-old man and the youngest of the Kawaguchi siblings. He has short black hair and pale skin. Like his brothers, he wears the sunshade of theWest Oasis Handyman Guildwith the kanji for the number three (三), being the youngest brother. His mask differs from his brothers in that mask's eyes look like small blue dots inside a dark rectangular area. All three brothers wear dark purple gloves, but Akio and Haruo's gloves have one square patch on the back of the hand. Under his mercenary clothes, he wears a white shirt. Haruo is a 17 year-old man and the youngest of the Kawaguchi siblings. He has short black hair and pale skin. Like his brothers, he wears the sunshade of theWest Oasis Handyman Guildwith the kanji for the number three (三), being the youngest brother. His mask differs from his brothers in that mask's eyes look like small blue dots inside a dark rectangular area. All three brothers wear dark purple gloves, but Akio and Haruo's gloves have one square patch on the back of the hand. Under his mercenary clothes, he wears a white shirt. In addition, on his back he wears a banner advertising himself as a handyman (便利屋). In the anime, he adds a "Reverse" symbol on the top right (裏) to represent their new affiliation. Haruo is the most shy of the three brothers, and he speaks with a stutter.

In addition, on his back he wears a banner advertising himself as a handyman (便利屋). In the anime, he adds a "Reverse" symbol on the top right (裏) to represent their new affiliation. Haruo is the most shy of the three brothers, and he speaks with a stutter.

characters.name: Hashimoto
characters.description: HashimotoEnglish nameHashVoice actorsJapaneseKenta MiyakeEnglishDoug BurksPersonal statisticsGenderMaleSpeciesHumanAppearancesDebut (anime)Master and Disciple Hashimoto(橋本), also known asHashin the English dub, is an elderly resident ofWater Station 30who appears inMaster and Disciple. WhenKosunavisits his house to clean and empty his septic tank, he asks her if she can deliver a package as well.MugenyatellsDesert Punkthat Kosuna did not clean the septic tank completely and also spilled human waste on Hashimoto.
